summary.what: Graceful hook deference: tiered environment classification, append-section strategy, hooks status command, init smart defaults, doctor messaging
summary.why: Timbers was stompy in multi-tool environments — backup-and-chain strategy took ownership of hook files, conflicted with beads core.hooksPath, and silent skips left agents unaware of options
summary.how: Four-tier environment classification (uncontested/existing/known-override/unknown-override), append-section replaces backup-and-chain, new hooks status command, tier-aware init/doctor messaging, old-format migration

notes: Debated whether to prompt for hooks in Tier 1 init — decided against it. Steering (Claude Code Stop hook) is the primary enforcement, git hooks are a bonus. Adding a second interactive prompt to init was a UX regression. Also debated .original vs .backup naming for the rename-and-chain fallback, then realized append-section eliminates the need entirely — refuse symlinks/binaries with guidance instead of maintaining a second code path. Council of reviewer + tester converged on all design decisions before implementation.
